name: What type of exterior door is best for coastal areas like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay?
headline: What type of exterior door is best for coastal areas like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay?

mainEntity.name: What type of exterior door is best for coastal areas like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: What type of exterior door is best for coastal areas like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: To choose the right exterior door for a foggy, windy, coastal climate like Pacifica or Half Moon Bay, focus on durability and weather resistance. Fiberglass and high-quality steel doors tend to hold up better than basic wood in salty, moist air. They resist swelling, warping, and peeling, which are common in coastal areas. Look for insulated cores, tight weatherstripping, and a good threshold to help block drafts and keep rain from blowing under the door. Doors with energy ratings designed for cooler, windy climates can help keep your home more comfortable and lower heating costs. If you love the look of wood, consider a fiberglass door with a wood-look finish, or be ready for regular maintenance like sanding, sealing, or repainting. Glass panels are fine, but double-pane, tempered glass will perform better and be safer. Pitfalls to avoid include choosing a door only for appearance, ignoring how it will behave in local weather. Cheap doors with weak finishes can start rusting or flaking quickly in ocean air. Also avoid undersized or poor-quality hardware; strong hinges, a solid deadbolt, and corrosion-resistant screws are important for both security and longevity. Before you buy, measure carefully, check the swing direction (inswing vs outswing), and confirm that the door size matches your rough opening so you don’t face costly modifications.
